premise: My father and stepmother intercepted the $124,000 life insurance payout my late mother left me, forged my signature, and bought a luxury RV to travel the country while I worked two jobs to finish college. Fifteen years later, when his dementia worsened and she begged me to pay for a private nursing home, I offered to buy the RV for $1,000 — then converted it into a mobile bar and made $80,000 the first year booking parties.
